name: Brute Force
summary: Check every combination of four indices with four nested loops — O(n⁴).

lineExplanations.3: Sort so each recorded quadruplet is in a canonical (non-duplicating) order.
lineExplanations.4: len(nums) gives the number of elements, n.
lineExplanations.5: Create an empty list to collect the answer quadruplets.
lineExplanations.6: First loop: choose the first index.
lineExplanations.7: Second loop: choose the second index, right of the first.
lineExplanations.8: Third loop: choose the third index, right of the second.
lineExplanations.9: Fourth loop: choose the fourth index, right of the third.
lineExplanations.10: Add the four chosen values to form the candidate sum.
lineExplanations.11: If the sum equals the target, this quadruplet is an answer.
lineExplanations.12: Append the four values to the results.
lineExplanations.13: Return every quadruplet that summed to the target.

syntaxExplanations.7: range(i + 1, n) starts just after i so indices never repeat.
